vulnerability.title: Ensure rsyslog-gnutls is installed
vulnerability.description: TLS protocol support for rsyslog is installed. The rsyslog-gnutls package can be installed with the following command: $ sudo dnf install rsyslog-gnutls
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if ! rpm -q --quiet "rsyslog-gnutls" ; then dnf install -y "rsyslog-gnutls" fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Installing the rsyslog-gnutls package provides TLS support for rsyslog, addressing the missing TLS protocol support identified in the STIG rule.

We need to use run_cmd for exactly one shell command at a time. We cannot chain. So we need to first check if package installed via rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls, capture output? We cannot store variable but we can decide based on exit code? The run_cmd returns something? Probably we can just run the install command directly; if already installed, dnf will say nothing to do and exit 0. That's acceptable? Might be fine. But better to check. However we are limited to one command per run_cmd invocation, but we can call multiple run_cmd sequentially. So we can first run rpm -q rsyslog-gnutls, then based on output decide to install. But we cannot branch in the tool usage. However we can just run install unconditionally; it's safe. The instruction: "Use run_cmd for EXACTLY ONE shell command at a time." Means each call is one command; we can make multiple calls. So we can call run_cmd with command "dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls". That's one command.

We should also maybe verify after install? Not required.

Thus we will output a single run_cmd invoking dnf install -y rsyslog-gnutls.
